title: Extract a defguard macro for a repeated range check
prompt:
The functions below all use the same guard expression `is_integer(n) and n >= 1 and n <= 99`:

```
def percent(n) when is_integer(n) and n >= 1 and n <= 99, do: {:ok, n}
def clamp(n) when is_integer(n) and n >= 1 and n <= 99, do: n
def above_10?(n) when is_integer(n) and n >= 1 and n <= 99, do: n > 10
```

Extract a `defguardp is_percent(n)` and use it in all three guards. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Percent do ... end`.

must_not_contain: when is_integer(n) and n >= 1 and n <= 99, do: {:ok

tier_rationale: Requires knowing `defguard`/`defguardp` (non-obvious for Sonnet which defaults to copy-pasting guard expressions).
